Question: At what values of $$x$$ is the function discontinuous? What condition of continuity is violated?
Answer: Discontinuous at $$x = - 1 ( \underset{ \left ( x \vec{} a \right ) }{lim} f ( x )$$ does not exist $$) , x = 3 ($$ jump discontinuity $$)$$ , and $$x = 7 ( \underset{ \left ( x \vec{} a \right ) }{lim} f ( x )$$ does not exist $$) .$$

Topic: Finding Limits: A Numerical and Graphical Approach
Problem Statement: For the following exercises, with the use of a graphing utility, use numerical or graphical evidence to determine the left- and right-hand limits of the function given as $$x$$ approaches $$a .$$ If the function has limit as $$x$$ approaches $$a ,$$ state it.  If not, discuss why there is no limit.
Question: $$f ( x ) = \{ \left .\begin{matrix}{aaa}| x | - 1 , & i f & x \ne 1 \\ x^{3} , & i f & x = 1\end{matrix}\right . \text{quad} a = 1$$

Topic: Finding Limits: A Numerical and Graphical Approach
Problem Statement: For the following exercises, with the use of a graphing utility, use numerical or graphical evidence to determine the left- and right-hand limits of the function given as $$x$$ approaches $$a .$$ If the function has limit as $$x$$ approaches $$a ,$$ state it.  If not, discuss why there is no limit.
Question: $$f ( x ) = \{ \left .\begin{matrix}{aaa}\frac{1}{x + 1} , & i f & x = - 2 \\ ( x + 1 )^{2} , & i f & x \ne - 2\end{matrix}\right . \text{quad} a = - 2$$

Topic: Finding Limits: A Numerical and Graphical Approach
Problem Statement: For the following exercises, with the use of a graphing utility, use numerical or graphical evidence to determine the left- and right-hand limits of the function given as $$x$$ approaches $$a .$$ If the function has limit as $$x$$ approaches $$a ,$$ state it.  If not, discuss why there is no limit.
Question: $$f ( x ) = \{ \left .\begin{matrix}{aaa}\sqrt{x + 3} , & i f & x < 1 \\ - \sqrt[3]{x} , & i f & x > 1\end{matrix}\right . \text{quad} a = 1$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the limits if $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} c \right ) }{lim} f ( x ) = -3$$ and $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} c \right ) }{lim} g ( x ) = 5.$$
Question: $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} c \right ) }{lim} ( f ( x ) + g ( x ) )$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the limits if $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} c \right ) }{lim} f ( x ) = -3$$ and $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} c \right ) }{lim} g ( x ) = 5.$$
Question: $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} c \right ) }{lim} \frac{f ( x )}{g ( x )}$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the limits if $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} c \right ) }{lim} f ( x ) = -3$$ and $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} c \right ) }{lim} g ( x ) = 5.$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the limits if $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} c \right ) }{lim} f ( x ) = -3$$ and $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} c \right ) }{lim} g ( x ) = 5.$$
Question: $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} 0^{+} \right ) }{lim} f ( x ) , f ( x ) = \{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}3 x^{2} + 2 x + 1 \\ 5 x + 3\end{matrix}\right . \text{quad} \left .\begin{matrix}{a}x > 0 \\ x < 0\end{matrix}\right .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, find the limits if $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} c \right ) }{lim} f ( x ) = -3$$ and $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} c \right ) }{lim} g ( x ) = 5.$$
Question: $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} 0^{-} \right ) }{lim} f ( x ) , f ( x ) = \{ \left .\begin{matrix}{a}3 x^{2} + 2 x + 1 \\ 5 x + 3\end{matrix}\right . \text{quad} \left .\begin{matrix}{a}x > 0 \\ x < 0\end{matrix}\right .$$

Question: $$\underset{ \left ( x \vec{} - 3 \right ) }{lim} ( \frac{\frac{1}{3} + \frac{1}{x}}{3 + x} )$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use numerical evidence to determine whether the limit exists at $$x = a .$$ If not, describe the behavior of the graph of the function at $$x = a .$$
Question: $$f ( x ) = \frac{- 2}{x - 4} ; a = 4$$
Answer: At $$x = 4 ,$$ the function has a vertical asymptote.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use numerical evidence to determine whether the limit exists at $$x = a .$$ If not, describe the behavior of the graph of the function at $$x = a .$$
Question: $$f ( x ) = \frac{- 2}{( x - 4 )^{2}} ; a = 4$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use numerical evidence to determine whether the limit exists at $$x = a .$$ If not, describe the behavior of the graph of the function at $$x = a .$$
Question: $$f ( x ) = \frac{- x}{x^{2} - x - 6} ; a = 3$$
Answer: At $$x = 3 ,$$ the function has a vertical asymptote.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use numerical evidence to determine whether the limit exists at $$x = a .$$ If not, describe the behavior of the graph of the function at $$x = a .$$
Question: $$f ( x ) = \frac{6 x^{2} + 23 x + 20}{4 x^{2} - 25} ; a = - \frac{5}{2}$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, use numerical evidence to determine whether the limit exists at $$x = a .$$ If not, describe the behavior of the graph of the function at $$x = a .$$
Question: $$f ( x ) = \frac{\sqrt{x} - 3}{9 - x} ; a = 9$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine where the given function $$f ( x )$$ is continuous. Where it is not continuous, state which conditions fail, and classify any discontinuities.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, with the aid of a graphing utility, explain why the function is not differentiable everywhere on its domain. Specify the points where the function is not differentiable.
Question: $$f ( x ) = \frac{x}{| x |}$$
Answer: The function would not be differentiable at however, 0 is not in its domain. So it is differentiable everywhere in its domain.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, with the aid of a graphing utility, explain why the function is not differentiable everywhere on its domain. Specify the points where the function is not differentiable.
Question: Given that the volume of a right circular cone is $$V = \frac{1}{3} \pi r^{2} h$$ and that a given cone has a fixed height of 9 cm and variable radius length, find the instantaneous rate of change of volume with respect to radius length when the radius is 2 cm. Give an exact answer in terms of $$\pi$$
